analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet cites a Chinese proverb about planning ahead and notes China's pledge to achieve carbon neutrality by 2060, inviting readers to see experts' views on how it will get there. Evaluation: CH1 (West immoral/hostile) – Not present; no mention of the West or hostility. CH2 (China as benevolent power) – Partially relevant thematically (climate action has international implications), but the tweet does not frame China as supporting global justice, multilateralism, or mutual benefits; it simply references a domestic target and expert views. Not sufficiently promoted. CH3 (epic history) – Not referenced. CH4 (appropriate political system and honorable values) – The proverb and target could imply foresight/values, but there is no explicit linkage to China’s political system or claims of moral governance; not promoted. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) – The tweet highlights a major national goal (carbon neutrality by 2060) and implies planning, but it does not claim achievement, leadership, or success, merely asks how China will get there. No success framing; not promoted. CH6 (culture/nature/heritage) – The proverb is cultural, but the tweet is not promoting culture per se; focus is climate policy. Conclusion: No listed narratives are clearly promoted.
